title: Cybernet Windows Setup Completion Flag
applies_to: Cybernet devices stopped at the Windows setup restart/error loop before final post-install app binding
preconditions: A technician is physically standing in front of the target Cybernet
The target is showing the Windows setup restart/unexpected-error dialog
The technician can open Command Prompt with Shift+F10
The target has not completed final post-install identity/app binding
forbidden_use: Do not use as a general Windows repair command
Do not use after final clinical app/device COM binding unless an operator explicitly approves it
Do not run silently from the admin box without a technician confirming the target screen state
scan_instructions: Open Command Prompt on the Cybernet with Shift+F10 from the Windows setup error screen
Scan the CMD QR payload into the Command Prompt window
Press Enter if the scanner does not send Enter automatically
Allow the device to restart and continue post-install
cmd_payload: reg add HKLM\SYSTEM\Setup\Status\ChildCompletion /v setup.exe /t REG_DWORD /d 3 /f && shutdown /r /t 0
powershell_payload: & reg.exe add HKLM\SYSTEM\Setup\Status\ChildCompletion /v setup.exe /t REG_DWORD /d 3 /f; shutdown.exe /r /t 0

qr_payloads.cmd_shift_f10: reg add HKLM\SYSTEM\Setup\Status\ChildCompletion /v setup.exe /t REG_DWORD /d 3 /f && shutdown /r /t 0
qr_payloads.powershell_console: & reg.exe add HKLM\SYSTEM\Setup\Status\ChildCompletion /v setup.exe /t REG_DWORD /d 3 /f; shutdown.exe /r /t 0

expected_result: After restart, Windows setup continues and post-install can finish on affected Cybernets.
rollback_note: No normal rollback is expected for this setup-state repair. Reimage if the setup state is invalid after the fix.
evidence_to_capture: target asset tag or hostname label
whether the device continued post-install after reboot
